description: Islamic State affiliate operating primarily in the mountainous regions of Puntland, northern Somalia. Formed in late 2015 when pro-IS fighters defected from al-Shabaab and pledged allegiance to Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi. The group controls a small, sparsely populated area and has an estimated 500–700 fighters. It is officially recognized by IS leadership as Somalia Province and is engaged in conflict with al-Shabaab and Puntland security forces.

country_links.evidence_quote: Bilal al-Sudani was responsible for fostering the growing presence of ISIS in Africa and funding the group's operations worldwide, including in Afghanistan.
country_links.context: The organization's leader Bilal al-Sudani funded the group's operations worldwide, including in Afghanistan.

country_links.evidence_quote: Australia has designated the militant as a terrorist organization, alleging that its experts, trainers, funds and weapons are being used to recruit members from local communities to collaborate with Somali pirates.
country_links.context: Australia has designated the militant as a terrorist organization, alleging that its experts, trainers, funds and weapons are being used to recruit members.

country_links.evidence_quote: In late 2019, ISS began releasing propaganda in Amharic, a language widely spoken in Ethiopia, in an attempt to attract new recruits there.
country_links.context: The organization releases propaganda in Amharic to attract recruits from Ethiopia.

country_links.evidence_quote: The United Nations also claimed in November 2017 that ISS receives direct assistance from IS officials in Syria and Iraq.
country_links.context: The organization receives direct assistance from IS officials in Syria and Iraq.

country_links.evidence_quote: While cells have periodically appeared in southern Somalia and Kenya
country_links.context: The organization has cells that have periodically appeared in Kenya.

country_links.evidence_quote: According to a New York Times report, Qatar may have financed and directed an Islamic State attack in Puntland on 10 May 2019...
country_links.context: Qatar may have financed and directed an Islamic State attack in Puntland.

country_links.evidence_quote: The United Nations also claimed in November 2017 that ISS receives direct assistance from IS officials in Syria and Iraq.
country_links.context: The organization receives direct assistance from IS officials in Syria and Iraq.

country_links.evidence_quote: The group is also directly supported by the Islamic State – Yemen Province, which is known to have sent experts, trainers, money, weapons and other materials to ISS.
country_links.context: The organization is directly supported by the Islamic State – Yemen Province, which sends experts, trainers, money, weapons, and other materials.
